summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a high‑energy first set with standout Jack Straw, Mama Tried and Peggy‑O.
review: The recording is a clean, crisp sound‑board source that lets the crowd’s excitement shine through. The first set rolls at upbeat tempos with tight Jerry solos on Jack Straw and Bertha, plus a killer Brent‑driven take on Peggy‑O/Masterpiece. While only the opening set is present, reviewers agree it represents one of the stronger shows from the 1988 MSG run.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are uniformly positive (4‑star) and highlight both strong musical performance and high‑quality soundboard audio, leading to a solid combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with no noticeable cassette hiss; the newer source fixes an earlier cut of Masterpiece and retains full fidelity throughout.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight, high‑energy first set that never drags; upbeat tempos and seamless transitions keep the momentum up. Only Set 1 is present on this disc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solos on Jack Straw and Bertha are highlighted as tight, clean, and a major reason to download.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland shines on the Peggy‑O/Masterpiece tease; reviewers call him "the man" for this song.
